evals.name: full-sweep
evals.expected_output: Full refactoring covering all coding-standards rules

assertions.trap: // Process orders is a terse label, not a doc comment explaining what the module does and how
assertions.description: Module/function starts with doc comment: what it does + how it works

assertions.trap: // Get user, // Process items, // Update cache describe mechanics, not consequences
assertions.description: Comments explain consequences, not mechanics. 'cache so the frontend...' not just 'update cache'

assertions.trap: big_orders table and 1000 threshold used without explaining the domain concept
assertions.description: Any domain terms explained on first use

assertions.trap: // Get user, // Notify, // Check stock are terse mechanical labels
assertions.description: Conversational comment tone, not mechanical

assertions.trap: throw new Error('blocked') exits early with no explanation of why blocked users are rejected
assertions.description: If any no-op/early-return, justified with why

assertions.trap: cache.set(`user:${userId}:lastOrder`, ...) with no comment on what next caller sees
assertions.description: Cache/DB state mutations explain what next caller sees

assertions.trap: return order.rows[0] with no documentation of what the caller should do with it
assertions.description: Return value documented: what the caller should do with it

assertions.trap: if (total > 1000) has no explanation of why 1000 is the threshold for big orders
assertions.description: The 1000 threshold for big orders explained with why

assertions.trap: export async function handleOrderData(...) has zero function doc naming consumer-visible behavior
assertions.description: Exported function has a function doc (~5 lines max) naming the consumer-visible effect / return-value meaning — NOT a bare label. @example is NOT required (the skill caps function docs at ~5 lines and defaults to no comment).

assertions.trap: handleOrderData uses banned words; u, usr, itm, ct, amt, s are cryptic abbreviations
assertions.description: handleOrderData renamed — no banned words (handle, data, process). Full words (usr→user, itm→item, ct→count, amt→amount, s→stock)

assertions.trap: sendEmail is a boolean without is/has/should prefix
assertions.description: Booleans use is/has/should prefix in positive form

assertions.trap: cache.set without corresponding cache.get; insert/update naming not symmetric
assertions.description: Naming pairs consistent (get/set, add/remove)

assertions.trap: isUrgent and sendEmail are boolean params that branch internal behavior
assertions.description: isUrgent and sendEmail boolean params split into separate functions or options object

assertions.trap: if (itm.qty > 0 && itm.active && !usr.blocked) nested inside for loop with inner if (amt > 0)
assertions.description: Nested if/else flattened to guard clauses, max 3 indent levels

assertions.trap: discountCode === 'FREE50' ? 50 : discountCode === 'FREE20' ? 20 : 0 nested ternary chain
assertions.description: discountCode if/else chain replaced with object map or switch

assertions.trap: let total = 0; total += amt; let ct = 0; ct++ mutate accumulators inside loop
assertions.description: No mutation of items array or item objects

assertions.trap: await db.query('SELECT qty FROM stock...') called sequentially inside for loop
assertions.description: Independent stock checks run in parallel (Promise.all)

assertions.trap: import { db }, import { cache }, import { mailer } are hardcoded global imports
assertions.description: db, cache, mailer injected via factory, not imported as globals

assertions.trap: handleOrderData mixes 7 concerns in one function: fetch user, loop-price items, check stock, big-order notification, create order, set priority, send email, update cache, log
assertions.description: handleOrderData is split by RESPONSIBILITY (not line count): at minimum fetch user, price items, persist order, and notify are separate named operations. The split is driven by mixing concerns, not by hitting an arbitrary line threshold.

assertions.trap: handleOrderData(userId, items, isUrgent, sendEmail, emailTo, taxRate, discountCode) has 7 positional args
assertions.description: 7 positional args replaced with options object

assertions.trap: Order creation, email sending, cache update, and logging all in one function
assertions.description: Order creation (command) separated from notification (side-effect)

assertions.trap: 1000, 50, 20 are magic numbers used inline without named constants
assertions.description: Magic numbers (1000, 50, 20) extracted to named constants

assertions.trap: userId: any, items: any[] accept anything; OrderStatus = string is untyped
assertions.description: Explicit types/interfaces for all params and returns, no any

assertions.trap: 'FREE50', 'FREE20' discount codes and 1000 threshold hardcoded in function body
assertions.description: Discount codes/thresholds in config, not inline

assertions.trap: No validation on qty (could be negative), items array (could be empty)
assertions.description: Input validation rejects invalid values (negative qty, empty items) — no ?? fallback

assertions.trap: throw new Error('not found'), throw new Error('blocked'), throw new Error('stock')
assertions.description: Errors returned as Result, no throw

assertions.trap: throw new Error('stock') is a bare string with no original cause or context
assertions.description: Errors preserve original cause/context

assertions.trap: itm.qty * itm.price * (taxRate ? 1 + taxRate : 1) - (discountCode === ...) is one compound expression
assertions.description: Compound expressions (tax calc, discount) extracted to named variables

assertions.trap: discountCode === 'FREE50' ? 50 : discountCode === 'FREE20' ? 20 : 0 nested ternary
assertions.description: Nested ternary for discounts removed, replaced with clear logic

assertions.trap: Dense code blocks with inconsistent spacing between logical sections
assertions.description: Logical blocks separated with blank lines

assertions.trap: // Log inside business logic comment labels dead-weight code that should be removed
assertions.description: Commented-out old approach removed

assertions.trap: No existing TODOs but refactoring should not introduce any without issue links
assertions.description: TODO/FIXME without tracking issue removed

assertions.trap: if (isUrgent)/else and if (sendEmail) branches have no journey narration
assertions.description: If any conditional branches, narrated with journey (optional — depends on refactoring approach)

assertions.trap: OrderStatus = string instead of union type; status 'pending' is a raw string
assertions.description: Make invalid states unrepresentable — union types for status, not bare strings

assertions.trap: userId: any, items: any[] — accepted without parsing into typed domain objects
assertions.description: Parse, don't validate — construct typed domain objects at boundary, reject invalid

assertions.trap: db.query calls have no timeout — can hang forever
assertions.description: Timeout on every I/O call

assertions.trap: console.log('Order created') inside business logic
assertions.description: No logging in business logic — use crosscutting wrapper (withLogging/withTracing)

assertions.trap: throw new Error('not found') — bare string, no type/code/status
assertions.description: Structured errors: { type, code, status, detail } not bare { message }

assertions.trap: return order.rows[0] — raw DB row returned to caller
assertions.description: Map DB entities to DTOs — dedicated response type, not raw rows

assertions.trap: Single export mixed in with all implementation
assertions.description: Exports/public API at top of file, private helpers at bottom

assertions.trap: formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice are 3 functions with identical params (to, name, amount, currency) and similar bodies — a pattern has emerged
assertions.description: 3 similar functions with identical signatures extracted into a shared abstraction or parameterized function

assertions.trap: to, name, amount, currency appear as the same 4 params across formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice
assertions.description: Repeated param group (to, name, amount, currency) extracted to a Value Object / named type

assertions.trap: order.customer.address.street, order.customer.address.city, order.customer.address.zip — 3 levels deep into object graph
assertions.description: Chained property access 2+ levels deep replaced with direct accessor or destructured at boundary

assertions.trap: validateOrder mixes high-level validation (check city exists, check total) with low-level string formatting (template literal with toFixed)
assertions.description: validateOrder split — validation logic separated from string formatting, no mixing of abstraction levels

assertions.trap: removeCartItem throws 'item not in cart' when removing an item that doesn't exist — but the postcondition is the same: item is not in cart
assertions.description: removeCartItem redesigned to succeed even if item absent — guarantees 'after call, item is not in cart' instead of throwing

assertions.trap: applyBulkDiscount forces callers to pass retryCount, retryDelayMs, maxRetries — retry logic pushed to caller instead of absorbed internally
assertions.description: Retry config params removed from applyBulkDiscount — module handles retry internally or removes it (caller shouldn't deal with retry complexity)

assertions.trap: if (total <= 0) throw new Error('invalid total') in validateOrder — a negative total after summing valid items is a programmer bug, but the code treats it like a normal error with no explanation
assertions.description: Impossible-state check returns Result error (not throw) AND has a comment explaining why this state should never occur — the comment documents the invariant

assertions.trap: Validation scattered: usr.blocked checked in main function AND redundantly inside loop (itm.qty > 0 && itm.active && !usr.blocked)
assertions.description: Validation consolidated at boundary — usr.blocked checked once at entry (barricade), not re-checked inside loop

assertions.trap: The order pipeline has 5+ sequential steps (validate user → check stock → calculate total → create order → set priority → notify → cache) explained only as a dense prose paragraph or not at all
assertions.description: The multi-step order pipeline (3+ steps) is documented as a module/function doc that lists the steps as a bullet list / step sequence (per the skill's mandatory module-doc shape: what, consumer consequence, how-as-bullets). An ASCII diagram is NOT required — the skill defaults to no comment and uses bullet lists, not diagrams.

assertions.trap: The top-of-file comment says 'Was originally a class-based OrderService, refactored to functions in Q3' — it narrates past implementation history
assertions.description: The 'refactored from a class in Q3' archaeology comment is removed. No comment references past implementation (no 'previously', 'used to', 'was', 'before', 'originally', 'refactored from')

assertions.trap: getInstanceConfig has a comment 'These values are exposed because the admin dashboard needs them on the instance settings page' — a shared API comment anchored to one specific caller (the admin dashboard / instance settings page) instead of the stable domain purpose
assertions.description: getInstanceConfig comment rewritten to describe the stable domain purpose of the config values (what they mean / what consumers decide with them) — NO reference to the admin dashboard, the instance settings page, or any single caller

assertions.trap: formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice, getShippingLabel, validateOrder, applyBulkDiscount, handleOrderData all mixed together — order fulfillment, document formatting, shipping, and validation are multiple unrelated business domains
assertions.description: The unrelated business domains are separated into clearly delimited sections/modules — order fulfillment, document formatting, shipping, validation are NOT interleaved or fused into one function. Since the prompt mandates all code inline in one file (no file writes), separation via distinct named sections + a noted split boundary (e.g. a comment 'belongs in its own module: shipping') counts; actual file split is NOT required here.

evals.name: file-structure-loc
evals.expected_output: Single workflow file with all single-use helpers inlined at the bottom

assertions.trap: validateEmail, formatWelcomeMessage, and hashPassword each live in their own file under src/utils/ but are imported from exactly one caller (signup-handler.ts)
assertions.description: The three single-use helpers (validateEmail, formatWelcomeMessage, hashPassword) are no longer in separate files under src/utils/. They are inlined at the call site or colocated at the bottom of the signup file. The three original utils files (email-validator.ts, welcome-formatter.ts, password-hasher.ts) are deleted.

assertions.trap: The signup feature is split across 4 files — signup-handler.ts, email-validator.ts, welcome-formatter.ts, password-hasher.ts — each with single callers
assertions.description: The signup feature lives in a single file (or at most 2 files if there's a legitimate responsibility split like infra types vs feature logic) — NOT scattered across 4+ files with single-caller helpers each

assertions.trap: A refactor under LoB rules must not introduce NEW helper files (shared/result.ts, shared/with-timeout.ts, etc.) that each have a single consumer — 'imagined future consumer' is not a justification
assertions.description: The refactor does not create new helper/primitive files (shared/*.ts, utils/*.ts, helpers/*.ts) that each have exactly one consumer. Result types, withTimeout, and similar 'shared primitives' are inlined into the caller's file until a second real consumer appears.
